_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Continuous Renal Replacement Therapy (CRRT)-Continuous Veno-Venous Hemodiafiltration (CVVHDF)
procedure_name: Continuous Renal Replacement Therapy (CRRT)-Continuous Veno-Venous Hemodiafiltration (CVVHDF) initiation cost for disposable
clinical_indications: Need for CRRT-Continuous Veno-Venous Hemodiafiltration procedure due to unstable hemodynamic status with clear justification for use over HD/PD/SLED

preauth.label: Clinical notes detailing history and evidence of unstable hemodynamic status along with clear justification of need to use CVVHD/DF over HD/PD/SLED
Indication for CRRT- Continuous Veno-Venous Hemodiafiltration procedure

claim.label: Clinical Photograph with the evidence of connections to the patient.

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailing history and Admission notes showing vitals and examination findings?
Were the patient's hemodynamic status and indications suggestive of the catheter placement?

common_queries: Missing clear justification for using CVVHD/DF over HD/PD/SLED in clinical notes
Clinical notes do not sufficiently detail history, admission notes, vitals, or examination findings
Patient's hemodynamic status or indications were not suggestive of catheter placement
Clinical photograph not showing clear evidence of connections to the patient
Barcode of the disposables used missing or unreadable at claim submission
eGFR or RFT report missing or incomplete at pre-authorization

clinical_key_pointers: Preparation phase: this phase consists of collection of necessary disposable material, identification and checking of the disposable set, set loading (cassette tubing), connection to the filter, positioning of the tubing, and hanging of bags.

Priming phase: priming solution is infused into the extracorporeal circuit in order to remove air and impurities remaining after sterilization of the set. When heparin anticoagulation is used, it is usually added to the priming solution. During this phase, the machine makes a general check of all components and sensors.
Connection to the patient: this phase consists of the connection of the extracorporeal lines to the patient's vascular access.
